programmes.show_description: (2021) Romantic drama with Odessa Young and Olivia Colman. In 1920s England a writer working as a maid has a passionate affair with the rich boy next door. It's not considered a good match.
Horse trainer Claire believes in country air and hard work. Her partner Bambi, a city-loving model and content creator, likes the easy life. Can Bambi keep up with Claire's schedule? (Ep3)
Can an eco-warrior and a petrolhead beauty salon owner agree to disagree on the environment? And a country-loving Yorkshireman shocks a London-based youth worker to the core. (Ep4)
Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer go house-hunting in Derby and Nottingham for single mum Jo and adrenaline junkies Liz and John, who want a home big enough for all their sporting equipment.
Last Exit to Springfield: Homer takes on Mr Burns in a labour struggle that rapidly spirals out of control after Homer leads the workers on strike.
So It Has Come to This: A Simpsons Clip Show: Bart's special April Fools' Day prank seriously misfires when the whole house explodes.
Pregnant Pause (Part 1/2): Carrie's shocked to discover that she's pregnant. And, with characteristic bad timing, Arthur wants Doug to build him a bathroom in the basement.
Pregnant Pause (Part 2/2): Doug struggles to hold down two jobs to save money for the baby's arrival. And Arthur still wants a new bathroom.
Screenless: The Simpsons have a digital detox, but one of them gets serious withdrawal symptoms - and, surprisingly, it's not Bart or Homer...
Better Off Ned: When Ned successfully mentors Bart, Homer gets jealous. So he mentors Nelson.
Neil Simpson is in contention for a medal in the Slalom final. Then the Men's Cross-Country Skiing Sitting 20km marks the culmination of Scott Meenagh's distinguished Paralympics career.
Neil Simpson, Sam Cozens and Fred Warburton fight for medals as the Men's Slalom reaches its climax. Dom Allen is in a medal hunt of his own in the Standing Slalom. Plus: Para Ice Hockey.
Mercedes secured a commanding one-two last time out in Melbourne, with George Russell taking the chequered flag. But Ferrari put up a spirited scrap. Can they challenge again in Shanghai?
The competition begins at Craig and Greta's Horseshoe Hotel in North Yorkshire. The guests love their eco approach to hosting but think that some of the facilities are lacking.
The B&Bers head to The Dixie Arms in Leicestershire, run by super-welcoming host Alex. Two guests are impressed by the blingy beds, but emotions run high at feedback time.
Matt hosts the week's third visit at Bradbury's of Blackpool, where he hopes to change perceptions of the seaside town. Will his supersized breakfast swing the competition in his favour?
The B&Bers go glamping at Little Oakhurst in Stonyhurst, near Clitheroe, run by Andy and Joanne. The views are spectacular, but it soon becomes apparent that glamping isn't for everyone...
There's plenty of score settling to be done on payment day as the B&Bers dissect the week, before the payments are revealed and the winners are announced.
Horseman David Jones tackles some bothersome bracken. Trebah Garden's head gardener creates a delightful stumpery. Nigel Cooper sails along the Dart for an annual competition.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
...Ceremony: Ade Adepitan, Billy Monger, Ed Jackson, Dame Sarah Storey, Sean Rose and Alice Tai present live coverage of the spectacular closing ceremony and look back at the Games.
The Capture, Married at First Sight Australia and Louis Theroux: Inside the Manosphere are some of the shows on this time. And there's also Will and Ralf Should Know Better.
(2022) Hugh Jackman, Vanessa Kirby, Laura Dern, Zen McGrath and Anthony Hopkins star in Florian Zeller's domestic drama about a father struggling to deal with his teenage son's depression.
La Frite: In Los Angeles, Gordon Ramsay visits La Frite, a French restaurant that's being run into the ground by its squabbling family owners.
Whacking Day: Lisa asks singer Barry White to help stop the cruel Springfield 'Whacking Day' tradition of hitting snakes with sticks.
Krusty Gets Cancelled: When Krusty's show gets axed, Bart and Lisa enlist Bette Midler, Hugh Hefner, the Red Hot Chili Peppers and Elizabeth Taylor to help him get it back.
Three rival couples compete in Cambridge, where stable owner Alice's feisty boyfriend Ollie reveals that he helped to hunt and butcher the deer for their main course...
To finally call their chapel home, Keith and Marj need a bedroom! Marj gilds a vintage mirror and Keith takes on storage solutions, before they enjoy a Welsh love spoon workshop.
In the prime commuter territory of Hertfordshire, Kirstie and Phil help newly-engaged Liz and Neil in Tring and David and Claire in St Albans.
Another chance to catch Friday's new episode, with Colin Jackson in Dictionary Corner. Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host.
Do Not Forsake Me, O My Postman: Cliff's ex-girlfriend Maggie returns to Cheers, heavily pregnant, claiming that the baby is his. Rebecca wants a jingle to advertise the bar.
Teaching with the Enemy: Lilith tells Frasier that she's been having an affair with a colleague. Sam hires a bouncer when bikers start drinking at Cheers.
The Girl in the Plastic Bubble: Frasier climbs out onto a high window ledge in protest when Lilith reveals that she plans to spend a year sealed in a biosphere with her lover.
Pet the Bunny: As a challenge, Ray writes a eulogy for his still-very-much-alive dad Frank.
Who Am I?: After Ray and Debra spend an evening apart, Ray admits that he no longer enjoys his juvenile pursuits with his friends.
Robert Needs Money: Ray and Debra lend Robert $1000 when he complains that he's struggling with his finances, but they're surprised when he spends it on a holiday to Las Vegas.
Desperately Seeking Closure: Frasier determines to find out what went wrong when Sam breaks off their relationship.
Perspectives on Christmas: Martin, Daphne, Niles and Roz each describe the tedious holiday season from their different perspectives.
Where Every Bloke Knows Your Name: The Fox and Whistle is Frasier's new local. But that's no fun for Daphne, who uses the pub as an escape from life's stresses.
The Zoo Story: Frasier gets a new agent, who sets him up with an opportunity for some great publicity when a crane at the local zoo is named after him.
Iberville Cuisine and Oyster Bar: Gordon helps Iberville Cuisine and Oyster Bar in New Orleans as they prepare for the Super Bowl being hosted in the city's Caesars Superdome.
Channel 4's midday news summary.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Tiverton, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet petrolhead Roger. Henry turns part of a tornado jet into a table, while Simon transforms a pair of exhaust pipes into trendy lamps.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: Matt Baker moves back to his family's organic sheep farm in the Durham hills. Renovating the miniature donkey stables is the first job on his to-do list.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Nicki Chapman in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Ben Hillman shows Mandy and Mick what the Costa Blanca has to offer for £240k, from beautiful balustrades to vivacious villas. But when each place surpasses the last, how will they choose?

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: Renovation rookies Angie and Ash dream big in the Italian Alps. In France, neighbours Aggi and Camille join forces to reopen a derelict restaurant.
The new run continues. Louise and Daga offer breathtaking views at their place in Cornwall. But forget what's out the window - the B&Bers are preoccupied with their tellies.
The new run continues. On night one in Bedfordshire and Cambridgeshire, self-described 'drunk Martha Stewart' Michelle shares some of her Portuguese and American culinary heritage. (Ep1/5)

programmes.show_name: New: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: Jean Johansson revisits Lisa and Rob's barn conversion in Charente. And in Andalucia, Jasmine Harman catches up with Alison and Paula... and their hot tub.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
Reform UK: Danny Kruger, MP for East Wiltshire, sets out Reform UK's plans to provide more support for Britain's veterans.
...Cooking for Less: Joe and Suzanne help single working mum of six Vicky to regain control in the kitchen. On the menu: a smoky bean cobbler, chicken parmigiana and more.
...Standing: The handcuffed pairs go on a road trip, away from home and family, just the two of them and the iconic, idyllic great British outdoors. Naturally, tempers flare. (Ep5)
The chemistry's strong between naked cleaner Elleigha and perfectionist chef Louis. Sisters Caitlin and Lucy have never dated before. And can joker Asher heal his past wounds?
